title: 10 best herbal teas for sunburn
intro: Herbal teas, such as chamomile and calendula, offer natural relief for sunburn. Chamomile is renowned for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which soothe redness and promote tissue healing. Calendula, on the other hand, contains flavonoids that reduce skin irritation, while its antimicrobial qualities prevent infection. Both teas create a gentle, herb-infused brew when steeped in hot water, offering a soothing topical treatment for sunburned skin.

teas.tea_desc: Chamomile tea helps with sunburn due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and swelling. Its antioxidant-rich composition aids in repairing skin damage caused by UV rays. Chamomile's soothing effect on the skin stems from its ability to accelerate skin cell and tissue regeneration. Additionally, chamomile tea possesses moisturizing qualities that alleviate sunburn-related dryness and irritation.
tea_parts: Flowers: Contain active compounds that possess anti-inflammatory properties, helping to soothe sunburned skin.
Leaves: Rich in antioxidants, leaves aid in skin repair and offer relief from sun damage.
Seeds: Packed with essential oils, seeds help to moisturize and reduce redness in sunburned areas.
tea_constituents: Apigenin: A flavonoid that possesses antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, reducing redness and swelling caused by sunburn.
Chamazulene: A chemical compound with potent anti-inflammatory effects, soothing sunburned skin and alleviating pain.
Bisabolol: A terpene alcohol known for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and skin-soothing properties, aiding in sunburn recovery.
Matricin: A precursor to chamazulene that has anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce sunburn-induced redness and irritation.
Flavonoids: A group of plant metabolites with antioxidant properties, protecting skin from harmful free radicals generated by sun exposure.
tea_recipe: Gather 2 cups of fresh chamomile flowers or 1 tablespoon of dried chamomile flowers.
Boil 4 cups of water and add the chamomile flowers, steep for 15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the flowers, let it cool down to a warm temperature.
Soak a clean cloth in the chamomile tea and apply it to the sunburned area for 15 minutes.
Repeat the application every few hours, storing the leftover tea in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

teas.tea_desc: Calendula tea treats sunburn due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and swelling. This herbal tea, derived from marigold flowers, contains antioxidants that aid in skin cell regeneration and help rebuild damaged skin tissue. Its soothing effect on the skin results from relieving pain and promoting faster healing of sunburn. Furthermore, calendula tea boasts antibacterial qualities that prevent infections in sunburned areas, safeguarding the skin from further harm. Lastly, drinking calendula tea internally contributes to overall skin health, enabling the body to better manage and recover from sun damage.
tea_parts: Flowers: Contain high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective for soothing sunburned skin.
Leaves: Rich in flavonoids and volatile oils, leaves help reduce redness and promote skin repair.
Stems: Contain beneficial polysaccharides, which have a calming effect on irritated skin caused by sunburn.
Seeds: Packed with essential fatty acids, seeds aid in moisturizing and healing damaged skin cells due to sun exposure.
tea_constituents: Flavonoids: Possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that reduce redness, swelling, and skin damage caused by sunburn.
Triterpenes: Accelerate healing by stimulating skin cell regeneration, repairing sun-damaged skin.
Carotenoids: Protect skin from free radicals and UV radiation, reducing the risk of sunburn and skin damage.
Polysaccharides: Provide a soothing effect and help maintain skin moisture, reducing discomfort and promoting skin repair.
Volatile oils: Contain therapeutic properties that alleviate pain and inflammation, helping to manage sunburn symptoms.
tea_recipe: Gather 1-2 tablespoons of dried calendula petals and place them in a tea infuser or loose in a teapot.
Boil 8 ounces of water and pour it over the calendula petals.
Steep the tea for 10-15 minutes to allow the calendula's healing properties to extract.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea to remove the petals.
Drink the tea while warm or allow it to cool and apply it directly to the sunburned area using a clean cloth.

teas.tea_desc: Peppermint tea provides relief for sunburn due to its anti-inflammatory properties derived from its menthol content. This natural substance reduces redness and inflammation by constricting blood vessels, thus soothing the skin. Moreover, peppermint tea contains rosmarinic acid, an antioxidant that helps in repairing skin damage caused by UV radiation. Furthermore, it boasts a hydrating effect, which aids in restoring moisture to the sunburned skin. Lastly, the cool temperature of the peppermint tea when applied topically offers instant refreshment, creating a numbing sensation that alleviates sunburn discomfort.
tea_parts: Leaves: Contain menthol, a natural cooling agent that soothes sunburned skin.
Flowers: Rich in antioxidants that aid in skin repair and healing of sun damage.
Seeds: High in flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties beneficial for treating sunburn.
tea_constituents: Menthol: Provides a cooling effect to soothe sunburned skin.
Rosmarinic acid: Possesses anti-inflammatory properties to reduce redness and swelling.
Flavonoids: Act as antioxidants to help repair damaged skin cells caused by UV radiation.
Tannins: Have astringent properties to help protect and heal sunburned skin.
Limonene: Offers antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that aid in sunburn recovery.
tea_recipe: Boil 8 cups of water, reaching a rolling boil for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1 tablespoon of dried peppermint leaves or 2 peppermint tea bags to the boiling water.
Steep the peppermint in the water for 5-7 minutes, creating a potent tea.
Strain out the peppermint leaves or remove tea bags, leaving only the tea.
Allow the tea to cool down to a warm temperature, then use it to gently clean and soothe sunburned skin.

teas.tea_desc: Lavender tea aids sunburn healing due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and swelling. This herbal tea contains antioxidants that help repair skin damage caused by sunburn. Furthermore, lavender tea boasts analgesic qualities that alleviate the pain associated with sunburn. The soothing effects of lavender tea on sunburn result from its ability to promote skin cell regeneration, enabling faster healing. Lastly, consuming lavender tea helps lower body temperature, providing relief from the heat generated by sunburn.
tea_parts: Flowers: Contain antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe sunburned skin.
Leaves: Rich in essential oils with skin-calming and anti-inflammatory compounds that aid in relieving sunburn discomfort.
Buds: High in active compounds, including linalool and linalyl acetate, which possess skin-healing and anti-inflammatory effects.
Stems: May contain trace amounts of beneficial compounds, although flowers and leaves are more potent for medicinal tea.
Seeds: Occasionally used in tea blends for their high lipid content, offering nourishment and hydration to sunburned skin.
tea_constituents: Linalool: A compound with anti-inflammatory properties that helps reduce redness and swelling caused by sunburn.
Linalyl acetate: A compound with analgesic properties that helps alleviate the pain and discomfort caused by sunburn.
Eucalyptol: A compound with cooling properties that helps soothe the burning sensation caused by sunburn.
Camphor: A compound with antiseptic properties that helps prevent infection in sunburned skin.
Beta-caryophyllene: A compound with anti-inflammatory properties that helps reduce inflammation and promote healing of sunburned skin.
Geraniol: A compound with antioxidant properties that helps protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which are produced in the skin after sunburn.
tea_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water, allowing it to reach a rolling boil for 2-3 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried culinary lavender to a tea infuser or teapot.
Pour the boiling water over the lavender, covering and steeping for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the lavender, discarding the used herbs.
Drink the lavender tea warm, up to three times a day, to help soothe and relieve sunburned skin.

teas.tea_desc: Witch hazel tea provides relief for sunburn due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which directly address the irritation and redness caused by overexposure to the sun. This herbal tea contains tannins, known for their ability to soothe skin and help restore its natural barrier. Furthermore, witch hazel possesses antioxidant qualities that aid in healing sun-damaged skin cells. Its astringent nature helps to reduce swelling and inflammation while promoting skin regeneration, ensuring a quicker recovery from sunburn. Ultimately, consuming witch hazel tea or applying it topically after sun exposure offers a natural remedy for alleviating the discomfort and symptoms associated with sunburn.
tea_parts: Leaves: Contain tannins that have an astringent effect, helping to soothe sunburned skin.
Bark: Rich in polyphenols and volatile oils, known for their anti-inflammatory properties, useful for relieving sunburn irritation.
Flowers: Packed with antioxidants, they help protect skin cells from damage caused by sunburn.
Buds: Contain high levels of gallic acid, a potent antioxidant that aids in skin repair and reduces redness from sunburn.
Rhyzomes: Full of flavonoids that help reduce inflammation and promote skin healing.
tea_constituents: Gallic acid: A potent antioxidant that helps reduce skin damage and inflammation caused by sunburn.
Tannins: Act as astringents, helping to soothe sunburned skin, reduce redness, and protect against infection.
Catechins: Possess anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate pain and swelling associated with sunburn.
Essential oils: Contain anti-inflammatory and analgesic compounds, promoting skin healing and providing relief from sunburn symptoms.
Saponins: Possess soothing and moisturizing properties that help to rehydrate and restore sunburned skin.
tea_recipe: In a pot, combine 2 cups of water and 2 tablespoons of dried witch hazel bark.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer, covered, for 15 minutes.
Remove pot from heat and let the tea steep for an additional 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the bark, then let it cool to a comfortable temperature.
Drink the tea up to three times a day to soothe sunburned skin.

teas.tea_desc: Helichrysum tea aids sunburn recovery due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and swelling. This herbal tea's antioxidants neutralize free radicals, promoting cellular repair and lessening sun damage. Furthermore, helichrysum tea boasts soothing qualities that alleviate pain and itching caused by sunburn. Its regenerative effects encourage skin cell regeneration, aiding in the quick healing of damaged tissue. Lastly, this tea's antibacterial properties prevent potential infections in skin lesions, ensuring optimal sunburn recovery.
tea_parts: Flowers: Contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce redness and irritation caused by sunburn.
Leaves: Rich in essential oils and flavonoids, helping to soothe and heal sunburned skin.
Stems: Contain tannins that can help reduce inflammation and promote skin repair after sun damage.
Buds: High in antioxidants and antimicrobial properties, helping to protect skin from further sun damage.
Seeds: Source of essential oils and fatty acids, promoting skin regeneration and easing discomfort from sunburn.
tea_constituents: Flavonoids: Possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce redness, swelling, and oxidative stress caused by sunburn.
Essential oils: Contain compounds like neryl acetate and geranyl acetate that have soothing and calming effects on sunburned skin.
Tannins: Act as astringents, helping to protect sunburned skin by forming a protective barrier and reducing inflammation.
Phenolic acids: Possess antioxidant properties that protect skin from free radical damage and promote skin repair after sunburn.
Terpenoids: Possess anti-inflammatory and wound-healing properties that soothe sunburned skin and promote tissue repair.
Rosmarinic acid: Acts as a potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, reducing skin damage caused by sunburn.
Caffeic acid: Has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to protect skin from UV damage and reduce swelling caused by sunburn.
tea_recipe: In a stove, bring 2 cups of water to a boil, taking approximately 5-10 minutes.
Add 2 tablespoons of dried helichrysum flowers to a teapot, then pour the boiling water over them.
Steep the tea for 10 minutes, allowing the healing properties of the helichrysum to extract.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the spent flowers.
Drink the tea while it's still warm, up to three times a day to soothe sunburned skin.

teas.tea_desc: St. John's Wort tea contains hypericin and hyperforin, compounds known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These substances reduce inflammation, helping to alleviate sunburn-induced redness and swelling. Furthermore, they neutralize free radicals produced by UV radiation, shielding skin cells from damage. The tea's soothing warmth enhances blood flow, facilitating the delivery of healing nutrients to affected areas. Consequently, St. John's Wort tea aids in sunburn recovery by reducing inflammation, neutralizing harmful free radicals, and promoting skin repair.
tea_parts: Flowers: Contain high levels of hypericin and hyperforin, which possess anti-inflammatory properties beneficial for soothing sunburned skin.
Leaves: Rich in tannins and flavonoids, which help reduce redness and alleviate discomfort caused by sunburn.
Buds: Packed with active compounds, these help accelerate skin repair and offer relief from sunburn-related irritation.
Stems: Contain valuable bioactive substances, which aid in minimizing sunburn symptoms and promoting skin recovery.
tea_constituents: Hypericin: Absorbs UV light, helping to reduce damage caused by sunburn.
Hyperforin: Possesses anti-inflammatory properties, helping to soothe sunburned skin and alleviate pain.
Flavonoids: Act as antioxidants, protecting skin cells from damage caused by free radicals generated during sunburn.
Essential oils: Provide a cooling effect, helping to alleviate discomfort caused by sunburn.
Tannins: Astringent properties help to reduce swelling and soothe irritated skin.
Phenolic acids: Offer antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, aiding in skin repair after sunburn.
tea_recipe: Infuse 2 teaspoons of dried St. John's Wort in 8 ounces of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea, discarding the spent herb.
Let the tea cool to a comfortable temperature.
Saturate a clean cloth with the tea and apply it to the sunburned area.
Repeat the application every few hours, storing the unused tea in the refrigerator.

teas.tea_desc: Yarrow tea aids sunburn recovery due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and swelling. This herbal tea contains compounds like azulene and flavonoids, known for soothing irritated skin and promoting tissue regeneration. Yarrow's antimicrobial qualities further help prevent infection in damaged skin. Moreover, its astringent nature constricts blood vessels, thereby alleviating pain and accelerating the healing process. Therefore, drinking yarrow tea or using it topically brings sunburn relief by addressing inflammation, promoting healing, and protecting against infection.
tea_parts: Leaves: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe sunburned skin.
Flowers: Rich in antioxidants that aid in skin regeneration and healing.
Seeds: Extracts have been shown to reduce redness and alleviate pain from sunburn.
Stems: Contain tannins that can help to draw out heat and reduce swelling.
Buds: High in essential oils that promote skin cell renewal and improve skin health.
tea_constituents: Flavonoids: Possess anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling caused by sunburn.
Alkaloids: Stimulate blood flow, aiding in the healing process of sunburned skin.
Sesquiterpene lactones: Exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, helping to alleviate sunburn damage and promote skin repair.
Tannins: Act as astringents, helping to protect sunburned skin by forming a protective barrier and reducing irritation.
Phenolic acids: Possess antioxidant properties that help protect skin cells from sun damage and support skin regeneration.
tea_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of fresh yarrow flowers and leaves, or use 1 tablespoon of dried yarrow.
Rinse the yarrow under cold water to remove any dirt or insects.
Place the yarrow in a teapot and pour 1 cup of boiling water over it.
Let the mixture steep for 10 minutes to extract the medicinal compounds.
Strain the tea, discard the solids, and drink the liquid up to three times a day to soothe sunburned skin.

teas.tea_desc: Plantain tea aids sunburn relief due to the anti-inflammatory properties of plantain leaves. These leaves contain compounds like allantoin, rosmarinic acid, and tannins that reduce inflammation, redness, and pain caused by sunburn. Furthermore, plantain tea promotes skin cell regeneration and helps to soothe irritated skin. Its rich antioxidant profile also combats free radicals generated by UV radiation, protecting the skin from further damage. As a result, plantain tea offers a natural remedy to alleviate discomfort and support skin healing after sun exposure.
tea_parts: Leaves: Contain antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe sunburned skin.
Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, flowers help reduce redness and irritation caused by sunburn.
Bark: Has tannins that can aid in healing damaged skin cells due to sunburn.
Seeds: Contain linoleic acid, which promotes skin barrier repair and helps soothe sunburned skin.
tea_constituents: Allantoin: Promotes healing of damaged skin cells.
Rosmarinic acid: Possesses anti-inflammatory properties that reduce redness and swelling.
Apigenin: Acts as an antioxidant, neutralizing free radicals caused by sun exposure.
Aucubin: Helps to reduce pain and inflammation in affected areas.
Chlorogenic acid: Improves skin hydration and moisture retention to soothe sunburned skin.
tea_recipe: Peel and chop 2 ripe plantains into small pieces.
Combine plantains with 4 cups of water in a pot, bringing to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer for 15 minutes, or until plantains are soft.
Strain the liquid into a container, discarding the plantain pieces.
Allow tea to cool, then store in the refrigerator for up to 3 days. Apply cool tea to sunburned skin using a clean cloth.

teas.tea_desc: Comfrey tea provides relief for sunburn due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and swelling. This herbal remedy contains allantoin, a substance known for healing and soothing damaged skin. Furthermore, comfrey tea boasts high levels of vitamin C and antioxidants that aid in repairing sun-damaged cells. Its demulcent quality forms a protective layer on the skin, alleviating pain and preventing further skin irritation. Ultimately, the regular application of comfrey tea on sunburned skin promotes faster healing and restores skin health.
tea_parts: Leaves: Rich in rosmarinic acid, allantoin, and flavonoids which possess anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and photo-protective properties, making them ideal for sunburn relief.
Rhyzomes: Contain high levels of allantoin, a potent cell proliferant and healing agent, aiding sunburn recovery.
Flowers: Packed with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds, fighting free radicals and reducing sunburn-induced redness and swelling.
Roots: Store high concentrations of mucilage, tannins, and rosmarinic acid, providing soothing, astringent, and anti-inflammatory effects for sunburned skin.
tea_constituents: Allantoin: Promotes new cell growth, helping to heal damaged skin caused by sunburn.
Rosmarinic acid: Possesses anti-inflammatory properties, reducing redness and swelling from sunburn.
Chlorogenic acid: Acts as an antioxidant, protecting skin cells from further sun damage.
Rutin: Strengthens blood vessels, reducing fluid buildup and inflammation in sunburned skin.
Quinic acid: Enhances skin barrier function, helping to lock in moisture and speed up the healing process.
Tannins: Act as a natural astringent, soothing pain and reducing itching from sunburn.
tea_recipe: Chop 1 cup of fresh comfrey leaves into small pieces.
Combine chopped leaves with 2 cups of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Steep the mixture, covered, for 30 minutes at room temperature.
Strain the tea, discarding the solid plant material.
Let the tea cool to a safe temperature before applying to sunburned skin.
